summary: Removed an empty fake function, fn_80386230, from sysdolphin baselib texpdag and folded its 4-byte symbol range into the preceding real function make_full_dependancy_mtx. The source stub was deleted and config/GALE01/symbols.txt now records make_full_dependancy_mtx as size 0x134 instead of 0x130, with HSD_TExpSchedule still starting at 0x80386234.
what_changed: Deleted the empty source stub `void fn_80386230(void) {}` from `src/sysdolphin/baselib/texpdag.c`.
Removed the separate `fn_80386230 = .text:0x80386230; // type:function size:0x4` symbol entry.
Updated `make_full_dependancy_mtx` in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` from size `0x130` to `0x134`, absorbing the 4 bytes previously assigned to the fake function.
Kept `HSD_TExpSchedule` starting at `.text:0x80386234`, so this was a boundary/symbol-size correction rather than a shift of later functions.
smart_moves: Resolved a fake one-instruction function by extending the preceding real function instead of keeping an artificial empty C function.
Kept source and symbol-map boundaries consistent: deleting the stub was paired with a matching symbol size update.
Made a minimal targeted cleanup with only two affected files and no unnecessary renaming or logic changes.
decomp_lessons: A tiny `fn_<address>` symbol, especially size `0x4`, adjacent to a known function can be a false function split rather than a real routine.
When removing a fake function, check whether its bytes belong to the preceding function and update the preceding symbol size accordingly.
Function-boundary fixes should preserve the next known function address unless there is evidence that later boundaries also changed.
Do not keep empty C stubs solely to satisfy an incorrect symbol split once the surrounding function size can account for the bytes.
naming_conventions: `fn_80386230` followed the generic address-based placeholder convention for unknown or fake functions; this PR shows such names should be removed when the symbol is proven non-real.
Existing baselib function names such as `HSD_TExpMakeDag`, `HSD_TExpSchedule`, `SimplifySrc`, and `SimplifyThis` remain unchanged.
`make_full_dependancy_mtx` retains the existing spelling used in the source/symbol map; the PR did not attempt a naming cleanup.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Folded a 4-byte fake function at `.text:0x80386230` into `make_full_dependancy_mtx` by changing its symbol size from `0x130` to `0x134`.
Deleted the corresponding empty C function so the source no longer creates an artificial symbol at the old boundary.
Maintained the following function boundary for `HSD_TExpSchedule` at `.text:0x80386234`.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No human body text or reviewer rationale was captured, so the inferred rationale is based on the title and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Find other size 0x4 `fn_` symbols adjacent to named functions that may be fake function splits.
Search `src/sysdolphin/baselib` for empty placeholder functions that only exist to satisfy current symbol boundaries.
Audit `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` around texpdag for additional suspicious one-instruction function entries.
